heading: Disable Read-Only Tools on Atlas
body:
If you no longer want to use read-only tools on your project, you can
turn this feature off in the project-level settings.

**Api:**

Atlas Administration API

To disable read-only tools with the Atlas Administration API,
send a `PATCH` request to the [settings
\<updateprojectsettings>\`endpoint with the
\`\`enableGenAIToolCalling\`](<https://www.mongodb.com/docs/api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-settings
\<updateprojectsettings\>`endpoint with the
``enableGenAIToolCalling`>) boolean flag value set to
`false`.

**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

To disable read-only tools for a project with the Atlas CLI,
specify `enableGenAIToolCalling: false` in a JSON configuration file:

```json
{
   "enableGenAIToolCalling": false
}
```

After you update the `enableGenAIToolCalling` field in your configuration
file, run the following command to update the project settings:

```
atlas api projects updateGroupSettings --file <path-to-config-file>
```

**Ui:**

Atlas UI
